'''Tom Lineham''' (born 21 September 1991), is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|wg}}er for [[Goole Vikings]] in the [[RFL League 1]].<ref name="England Knights v Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights v Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=24 October 2019}}</ref>

He has previously played for the [[York City Knights]] in [[RFL League 1|Championship 1]], and [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]]. He has spent time on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in [[2016 League 1|League 1]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Background==
Lineham was born in [[Leeds]], [[West Yorkshire]], England. He grew up in South Milford and attended Sherburn High School and York College.

==Career==
===York RUFC===
Lineham's position as a junior with [[York RUFC]] was in the [[Rugby union positions#Back row (Loose forwards)|back row]], he was equally adapt at [[Rugby union positions#Number Eight|number 8]] or openside-[[Rugby union positions#Flanker|flanker]], but has played as an inside-[[Rugby union positions#Centre|centre]] for York RUFC, and more recently as a {{rlp|wg}} when he converted to [[rugby league]].{{cn|date=October 2022}}

Lineham joined [[York RUFC]] under 15s in September 2006 as a raw young talent with good speed and strength. He quickly became a regular in the York team, going on to represent North Yorkshire that same season. Lineham's second season with York was an even better one than his first, he was York's top tryscorer at the end of the season, which led to a Yorkshire call up at blindside-[[Rugby union positions#Flanker|flanker]] part way through the 2007-2008 season.{{cn|date=October 2022}}

Lineham then went to [[York College (York)|York College]] in 2009 to study for his A-Levels, this is when he first started playing rugby league for the College's Rugby Development Centre, originally coached by the late Paul Higgins. Lineham scored 15 tries in 7 appearances, earning a trial with the Knights. Lineham played two trial games with the Knights reserve grade and subsequently was offered an 18-month contract with the Knights in 2009. Lineham has signed a further 1-year contract at the Knights for the season 2011 season where the Knights will be playing in the championship after last seasons promotion. Hull F.C. were believed to have approached Lineham with an offer but no contract was agreed due to Linehamneeding [[anterior cruciate ligament]] (ACL) knee reconstruction leaving in him sidelined for 7 months.{{cn|date=October 2022}}

Linehams first try for the Knights first team came on 2 April 2010 against [[Newcastle Thunder|Gateshead Thunder]] at [[Huntington Stadium]] when he ran the ball in from 50-metres.{{cn|date=October 2022}}

===Hull FC===
Lineham signed for Hull F.C. in 2011. After a number of good performances in the Academy, Lineham made his first team début as a late substitute in the 32-18 derby win at Craven Park. His performance was rewarded with a starting spot in the next game at home to [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]], where he scored two tries whilst making a notable contribution to another, in a Man of the match performance.{{cn|date=October 2022}}

His breakthrough season was in 2013 in which he was named the Hull FC's Young Player of the Year and the Super League Albert Goldthorpe Rookie of the Year.

Lineham has previously played for the [[York City Knights]]. He has also played [[rugby union]] for York RUFC. Lineham represented Yorkshire in Rugby Union.<ref>{{cite news
|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/8158324.stm
|title=Ratcliffe joy muted after victory
|date=20 July 2009|work=BBC Sport
|publisher=BBC
|access-date=8 December 2010}}</ref>
In 2013 Lineham was awarded the Albert Goldthorpe Rookie of the Year Medal.<ref>{{cite web|title=The Albert Goldthorpe Awards|url=http://www.totalrl.com/web/the-albert-goldthorpe-awards-roll-of-honour/|work=totalrl.com|publisher=League Publications|accessdate=16 February 2014}}</ref>
[[File:Tom Lineham.jpg|thumb|right|Lineham playing for Hull FC in 2014]]
Lineham enjoyed another outstanding season for Hull in 2015, scoring 26 tries.

===Warrington Wolves===
In 2016, he moved to play for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], where he had a turbulent start and although continued his formidable try scoring record he was dropped from the team and Super League. He returned for the league leaders decider against his former Team [[Hull F.C.]] scoring a brace in a solid individual performance. It is believed that Lineham had ambitions of playing for [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].<ref name="Warrington's Tom Lineham faces further fall from grace following poor Super League form">{{cite web|url=http://www.express.co.uk/sport/othersport/662455/Warrington-Wolves-Rochdale-Hornets-Tom-Linehan-Super-League-News|title=Warrington's Tom Lineham faces further fall from grace following poor Super League form|publisher=express.co.uk|date=31 December 2016|accessdate=1 January 2017}}</ref>

He played in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|accessdate=7 November 2019}}</ref>

In the last year Lineham's weight gain and consequent loss of speed has earned him the comical if not somewhat cruel nickname, "the flying pig". A nickname which he apparently endorses.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/18290737.interview-warrington-wolves-winger-tom-lineham/|title=Interview with Warrington Wolves winger Tom Lineham|website=www.warringtonguardian.co.uk}}</ref>
He played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Catalans Dragons]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|publisher=Guardian|date=25 August 2018|accessdate=5 November 2019}}</ref>
He played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=13 October 2018|accessdate=5 November 2019}}</ref>
[[File:Tom Lineham Warrington Wolves Anfield 2019.jpg|thumb|right|Lineham (centre) playing for the Warrington Wolves at [[Anfield]] in 2019]]
He played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|accessdate=1 November 2019}}</ref>

On 14 September 2020, Lineham was charged by the [[Rugby Football League|RFL]] and was given a Grade F "other contrary behaviour", (the most serious grade on the disciplinary panel, which carries a minimum of 8 games suspension.) for an alleged "[[squirrel grip]]" on [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]]'s [[Alex Foster (rugby league)|Alex Foster]]. He appeared before a disciplinary hearing on 15 September 2020, to find out the length of his suspension. Lineham contested the decision of his grade, but the tribunal dismissed his decision and he was found guilty of a Grade F and was suspended for 8 games, and fined £500. Depending on Warrington's progression in the [[Challenge Cup]], he would have potentially missed the remainder of the season.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/news/18723235.warrington-wolves-winger-banned-squirrel-grip/|title=Lineham banned for 8 matches|date=15 September 2020  |website=Warrington Guardian}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
On 17 August 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Sky Sports|date=17 Aug 2021|accessdate=13 Oct 2021|title=Wakefield Trinity sign Tom Lineham and Liam Hood for 2022 Super League season|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12208/12383542/wakefield-trinity-sign-tom-lineham-and-liam-hood-for-2022-super-league-season}}</ref>
Lineham played 12 matches for Wakefield Trinity in the [[Super League XXVIII]] season as the club finished bottom of the table and were relegated to the [[RFL Championship]] which ended their 24-year stay in the top flight.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/20876/12962152/super-league-wakefield-trinity-relegated-after-loss-at-leigh-leopards-st-helens-and-wigan-warriors-win/|title=Super League: Wakefield Trinity relegated after loss at Leigh Leopards, St Helens and Wigan Warriors win|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

===York Knights (re-join)===
On 13 Mar 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[York Knights]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a 1-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=13 Mar 2024|author=Aaron Bower|title=York Knights confirm return of Tom Lineham on eve of Championship season|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/york-knights-confirm-return-of-tom-lineham-on-eve-of-championship-season}}</ref>

===Goole Vikings===
On 24 July 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Goole Vikings]] in the [[RFL League 1]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=24 Jul 2025|accessdate=1 Aug 2025|author=Stephen Ibbetson|title=Former Super League star comes out of retirement to join Goole Vikings|url=https://www.totalrl.com/former-super-league-star-comes-out-of-retirement-to-join-goole-vikings/}}</ref>

==International career==
In 2018 he was selected for the [[England Knights]] on their tour of [[Papua New Guinea]]. He played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] at the Lae Football Stadium.<ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718006#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=27 October 2018|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref> Lineham also played against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|PNG]] at the Oil Search National Football Stadium.<ref name="Papua New Guinea v England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718007#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea v England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=3 November 2018|accessdate=29 October 2019}}</ref>

In 2019 he was selected for the England Knights against [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]].<ref name="England Knights vs Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights vs Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref>
